sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Madrid have shown strong form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, including a convincing 3-0 victory over Athletic Club, though they suffered a 0-2 home loss to Celta Vigo. Sevilla's form has been inconsistent with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5 matches, with a notable 4-0 win against Real Oviedo but a recent 1-0 loss to Deportivo Alavés. Madrid's attacking prowess and consistency give them an edge going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Real Madrid have dominated Sevilla, winning both matches with scores of 4-2 at home and 2-0 away. Historically, Madrid have not lost to Sevilla in their last 13 meetings, with both teams often scoring in recent clashes (7 of 9 matches). This trend suggests Madrid's continued superiority in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Real Madrid's Kylian Mbappé is in exceptional form, scoring 5 goals in his last 5 matches, while Jude Bellingham remains a threat with a 7.6 average rating against Sevilla. Sevilla miss key attacking players like Akor Adams and Ruben Vargas, which could hinder their offensive output. Madrid's missing defenders like Éder Militão may slightly weaken their backline, but their depth compensates.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Madrid rank 2nd in La Liga with 34 goals scored and only 16 conceded, boasting a high expected goals difference (+6.02). Sevilla, sitting 9th, have struggled defensively, conceding 24 goals while scoring the same amount. Madrid's superior possession (59%) and chance creation (60 big chances) highlight their dominance over Sevilla's less consistent stats.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Real Madrid's strong form, historical dominance over Sevilla, and the Sofascore model's 76% probability of a home win, a victory for Madrid seems likely. Sevilla's defensive vulnerabilities and missing players further tilt the balance. Expect a high-scoring game, with Madrid likely to exploit their attacking strength at the Bernabéu.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Real Madrid leads Sevilla 1-0 at the Bernabéu in Madrid. The home side took the advantage through a well-taken header by Jude Bellingham in the 38th minute. Real Madrid has dominated much of the game, controlling possession and creating more clear-cut opportunities. Sevilla, while showing some resilience, has struggled to impose themselves consistently and will need to find a way to turn the tide in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid has been the superior side in the first half, dictating the tempo with a higher share of possession and generating multiple big chances. Their entries into the final third and penalty area have been frequent, showcasing their attacking intent. Defensively, they’ve been strong in duels, particularly in the air, limiting Sevilla’s opportunities. On the other hand, Sevilla has had moments of promise, with a few entries into dangerous areas, but their inability to convert chances and lower success rate in duels has left them trailing. They’ve also picked up more disciplinary cards, which could impact their approach in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: Jude Bellingham has been the standout for Real Madrid, not only scoring the crucial goal but also contributing significantly in midfield with his energy and precision. Rodrygo has been instrumental in creating opportunities, providing the assist for the goal and showing creativity in the final third. For Sevilla, players like Alexis Sánchez have tried to spark attacks with key contributions, but the lack of finishing has hurt them. Isaac Romero showed potential but missed a big chance, summing up their first-half struggles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Real Madrid largely in control of the attacking momentum, with several sustained spells of pressure, particularly in the middle and latter stages of the half. They had consistent periods of dominance, building up attacks with intent. Sevilla, however, managed a few notable attacking phases, especially early on and towards the end of the half, showing they can threaten when given the chance. The momentum often swung back to the home side, who looked more dangerous overall.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid’s approach has been to dominate possession and push forward with numbers, exploiting spaces in Sevilla’s defense. Their high pressing and ability to win possession in the final third have kept Sevilla on the back foot. Sevilla, meanwhile, has relied on counter-attacks and quick transitions, but their execution has been lacking. They’ve struggled to win duels, particularly in the air, which has hindered their ability to retain possession and build sustained pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Real Madrid appears poised to maintain their dominance if they continue with the same intensity. They are likely to push for more goals, capitalizing on their attacking options and home advantage. Sevilla will need to tighten up defensively and take more risks going forward if they are to get back into this game. A potential equalizer could shift the momentum, but they must improve their finishing and win more duels to stand a chance.
